text: When designing a REST API, following best practices ensures it is easy to use and maintain. First, choose clear and consistent endpoint names. For example, use `/users` for user resources and `/users/123` for a specific user. Second, use proper HTTP status codes to indicate the result of a request, such as `200` for success, `404` for not found, and `500` for server errors. Always include versioning in your API, like `/v1/users`, to avoid breaking changes for existing users. Finally, write clear and complete documentation, explaining endpoints, examples, and error messages. Good documentation helps developers integrate quickly and reduces support questions.
